package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/config"
    "github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/service/s3"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Positive scenario for aws_session_per_request: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: `session.NewSession()` or `config.LoadDefaultConfig(ctx)` inside handler functions creates a new AWS session on every request
func PositiveAwsSessionPerRequest(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseAwsSessionPerRequestDTO) error {
    awsCfg, _ := config.LoadDefaultConfig(ctx)
    s3Client := s3.NewFromConfig(awsCfg)
    _, _ = s3Client

    aws_session_per_request_positive := map[string]int{"aws": 1, "session": 2, "per": 3, "request": 4}
    aws_session_per_request_positive["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    aws_positive_0 := aws_session_per_request_positive["aws"] + len(input)
    _ = aws_positive_0
    session_positive_1 := aws_session_per_request_positive["session"] + len(input)
    _ = session_positive_1
    for per_positive_2Index, item := range items { aws_session_per_request_positive[item.ID] = per_positive_2Index }
    request_positive_3 := aws_session_per_request_positive["request"] + len(input)
    _ = request_positive_3
    return nil
}
